draft.introduction: The Tirukuṛaḷ by Thiruvalluvar is the masterwork of Tamil ethical literature. Its 1,330 couplets cover virtue, wealth, and love with epigrammatic precision.

translation.text: As the letter A is the first of all letters, so the primeval God is first in the world. What has learning profited a man if it has not led him to worship the good feet of Him who is pure knowledge?
translation.original_text: akara mutala ezhuttellaam aati pakavan mutatte ulakku

translation.text: The household life of the man who acts rightly in his domestic role is higher than the life of the ascetic who has renounced the world. What is the use of shaven heads and matted locks if one does not refrain from deeds that wise men know to be wrong?
translation.original_text: illaram alla ellaam nanru aal udaiyaan

translator_notes.note: The Tirukuṛaḷ is unusual in classical Indian literature for being claimed simultaneously by Hindus, Jains, and Christians as compatible with their traditions. Its ethical universalism transcends sectarian categories.
